product_summary.one_sentence_summary: Luxembourgish training for employees and candidates who need workplace integration or official-language progress in Luxembourg.
product_summary.target_user: Expat employees, cross-border workers and public-sector candidates who need usable Luxembourgish.
product_summary.core_pain: People need a structured way to improve Luxembourgish while navigating official leave, certificate and acceptance rules.
product_summary.claimed_outcome: Workplace and integration-oriented Luxembourgish progress with a path toward recognized proof where available.

primary_buyer_type.reason: The course can be bought by private learners, even if employers may also support it.

secondary_buyer_types.reason: Employers may sponsor Luxembourgish learning for integration and retention reasons.
Companies with integration-heavy workforces may buy the course for staff.

budget_owner.reason: The payment can come from the learner, the employer, or an official leave-support route depending on the case.

buyer_influencers.reason: HR teams influence language support for integration and onboarding.
Managers care whether the learner can function in workplace Luxembourgish.
Official leave and proof rules make legal or compliance review relevant in some cases.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Congé linguistique provides up to 200 hours of paid linguistic leave for Luxembourgish courses.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: Guichet.lu states that linguistic leave is limited to 200 hours and is divided into two stages across a professional career.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Eligible Luxembourgish courses must be provided by recognised institutions, approved associations, chambers, ministries or public bodies.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: The official leave page lists the categories of institutions whose Luxembourgish courses are eligible.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: INLL is the national certification body for Luxembourgish diplomas and certificates.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: INLL states that it is recognised as the national certification body for Luxembourgish language diplomas and certificates.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: INLL courses do not give a certificate valid for a nationality application.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: INLL explicitly states that its Luxembourgish courses do not grant a certificate valid for a nationality application.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: The product's CEFR certification is sufficient for official needs.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: A provider-issued certificate and official acceptance are not the same thing, and no public source reviewed validates this product-specific claim.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: INLL is free, overcrowded and generic.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: The public sources reviewed show free self-learning and broad course offerings, but not an objective measure of overcrowding.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: Luxembourgish demand is rising because of integration requirements.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: This is plausible context, but the reviewed sources do not quantify demand growth for this product.

regulator_entities.name: Ministry of Labour
regulator_entities.relevance: Processes linguistic leave applications in Luxembourg.

regulator_entities.name: INLL
regulator_entities.relevance: Recognised as the national certification body for Luxembourgish language diplomas and certificates.

funding_bodies.name: Ministry of Labour linguistic leave service
funding_bodies.relevance: Pays or reimburses the salary-compensation mechanism behind linguistic leave.

funding_bodies.name: INFPC
funding_bodies.relevance: May matter for employer-funded training, though the leave route is the cleaner fit here.

approval_entities.name: INLL certification route
approval_entities.relevance: The official Luxembourgish certification route matters if the product wants formal proof value.

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Linguistic leave is capped at 200 hours and must be split into two stages of 80 to 120 hours each.
regulatory_requirements.applies_to: Employees and self-employed applicants in Luxembourg

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Only Luxembourgish courses from eligible institutions can be used for linguistic leave.
regulatory_requirements.applies_to: Applicants using the linguistic leave route

subsidies_or_funding.name: Linguistic leave
subsidies_or_funding.eligibility_notes: The route is directly relevant, but only if the course provider fits the official eligibility categories.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: INLL Luxembourgish courses
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Public Luxembourgish course offering with official certification role.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: Prolingua Luxembourgish courses
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Private Luxembourgish and tailored language courses, including company delivery.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: inlingua Luxembourg
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Company and individual language courses that can substitute for some workplace-integration language needs.

legal_claim_severity.reason: The product uses a formal training-aid route and official-language proof logic, but not a licensed-practice claim.

authority_needed_for_delivery.reason: The course can be delivered without formal authority, though eligibility for leave support needs validation.

authority_needed_for_claims.reason: Claims about official proof, leave support and certification need external validation to stay credible.

proof_stack.reason: The product must win against free public alternatives and local private schools.
This category depends on accepted proof or certification logic, not just participation.
Learner progression is still an important intermediate proof signal.

proof_burden.reason: The route is official and proof-sensitive, but the product remains a training offer rather than a licensed profession pathway.

dependency_types.reason: The linguistic leave route is part of the value proposition.
The offer is deeply tied to Luxembourg institutions and leave rules.
Luxembourgish teaching capacity is a core operational dependency.
If the product wants official proof value, accepted certification matters.

scope_variance_risk.reason: The current scope is Luxembourg-only, but public and private proof routes differ inside that scope.

expansion_variance_risk.reason: Outside Luxembourg, both the language value proposition and the leave logic would change materially.

subsidy_exists.reason: Linguistic leave clearly exists as a public support route for Luxembourgish learning.

subsidy_eligibility_status.reason: The support route exists, but the provider must still fit the official course-eligibility categories.

subsidy_conflict_flag.reason: No direct policy conflict was found, only provider-eligibility dependence.

market_regulatory_context.reason: This is language training, but with a formal leave and certification layer.

claim_language_risk.reason: Official proof and certificate wording must be handled carefully because INLL distinguishes between course learning and nationality-valid certification.

authority_dependency_risk.reason: The product depends on official leave-eligibility and certification logic.

competitive_gap_risk.reason: Public and private Luxembourgish offerings already exist, including an official certification body and private substitutes.

numeric_claim_risk.reason: The 200-hour leave figure is official and cleanly stated.

subsidy_conflict_risk.reason: The support route is real, but course eligibility depends on provider status.

positioning_risk.reason: Without a strong official-proof angle, the product can blur into generic Luxembourgish instruction.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: The product's CEFR certification is sufficient for official needs.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: INLL explicitly distinguishes between course offerings and certificate value for nationality, so a provider-issued CEFR report should not be treated as official proof without validation.
The public evidence shows free and broad offerings, but not an objective overcrowding measure.

recommended_manual_review.reason: Decide whether the product will sell on official certification value, workplace progress, or both.
Check if the chosen course provider is actually eligible for linguistic leave support.
Avoid implying that a provider-issued certificate is automatically equivalent to official recognition.

sources.title: Learn to speak Luxembourgish with our language courses
Language courses that focus on the power of dialogue
Initiation à la langue luxembourgeoise dans le contexte professionnel
Tailor-made courses in your company, at Prolingua's premises or at a distance
Aides à la formation professionnelle continue en entreprise
Institut national pour le développement de la formation professionnelle continue (INFPC)
